title: metopium brownei
intro: Metopium brownei, also known as West Indian or Obeche poison tree, is a plant species native to the Caribbean and Central America, characterized by its toxic sap and distinctive heart-shaped leaves. The sap of the Metopium brownei plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antifungal properties. The health benefits of Metopium brownei include the treatment of various ailments such as skin conditions, respiratory issues, and wounds, although it should be noted that its use is often in a diluted form. Herbal preparations of Metopium brownei typically involve the sap being extracted and applied topically or used in traditional remedies, but its use should be approached with caution due to its toxic nature.
